description: Retry of an Idempotency-Key publish with the same (agent_id, key) AND the same content_hash as the original successful publish. Registry MUST return the stored response with HTTP 200 OK (NOT 201) and the original assigned identifiers (ctx_id, lineage_id, version, created_at, status). Registry MUST NOT re-execute the full §2.1 pipeline — the lookup short-circuits per RFC-ACDP-0003 §6.2.1 step 2.

existing_record.stored_response_summary: The 201 Created response body returned for idem-001, including the registry-assigned ctx_id.

input.notes: Body bytes MAY differ in non-canonical whitespace from the original submission, so long as the recomputed content_hash equals sha256:H1. The registry's lookup is keyed on the producer-supplied content_hash header field, which is identity-of-content for the canonicalized body.

expected.response_body: Byte-identical to the response stored for idem-001 (same ctx_id, same lineage_id, same created_at, same status, same version).
expected.rationale: The whole point of Idempotency-Key is to make safe retries cheap. A registry that re-runs the full pipeline on every retry is conformant only if it returns the same identifiers — and the simplest way to guarantee that is to short-circuit on lookup.
